market_note: April 19 is a Saturday; data reflects Friday April 17 close (most recent trading day)
key_themes: Iran/Strait of Hormuz ceasefire relief rally
Analyst upgrades in tech and fintech

nasdaq.note: 13th consecutive daily gain — longest streak since 1992

stocks.reason: Q1 2026 revenue beat (+16% YoY to $12.25B) but Q2 guidance missed consensus; stock fell ~10% after-hours. Co-founder Reed Hastings also announced he is stepping down after 29 years.

Surged ~30% for the week on massive AI momentum — remaining performance obligations hit $553B (up 325% YoY), including contracts with OpenAI, Meta, and xAI. Additional catalyst: a deal to procure 2.8 GW of power from Bloom Energy for AI data centers.
Surged 9.7% as Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz fully open under ceasefire, driving a broad travel and consumer discretionary rally.
Jumped more than 9% alongside the broader travel rally triggered by the Iran ceasefire and reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
Gained approximately 5% as geopolitical relief from the Iran ceasefire boosted the entire travel sector.
Rose ~1.4% after Reuters reported iPhone shipments in China jumped 20% in Q1 2026; Bank of America reiterated Buy, calling it the 'highest quality name' and a 'high-quality compounder.'
On pace for its 11th consecutive winning session — the longest daily win streak on record — amid ongoing AI infrastructure investment enthusiasm and recurring positive analyst coverage.
Soared 22% after Oracle announced a deal to procure up to 2.8 gigawatts of on-site fuel cell capacity from Bloom Energy to power AI data centers, plus a $400M warrant.
UBS upgraded Tesla to Neutral from Sell, citing that current share levels 'more evenly balance' near-term demand challenges with its long-term physical AI opportunity.

Argus initiated coverage with a Buy rating and $520 price target; Wells Fargo raised its price target to $560 (from $543), keeping Overweight, citing strong double-digit revenue growth and margin expansion.
Wolfe Research initiated coverage at Outperform, noting the company has a differentiated AI cloud infrastructure offering; also featured in CNBC's Thursday top analyst calls.
Jumped more than 3% after Morgan Stanley named it a top pick, citing potential for significant earnings upside in the buy-now-pay-later space.
Fell more than 5% as oil prices plunged 12%+ following Iran's declaration that the Strait of Hormuz is 'completely open'; one of the largest one-day drops in the energy sector in a year.
Dropped approximately 7% as the Iran ceasefire and sharp oil price decline triggered a sector-wide selloff in energy stocks.
Shed more than 9% — one of the worst performers in the energy selloff — as WTI crude futures posted their sharpest single-session decline since April 2020 on ceasefire news.
Featured in Friday analyst calls ahead of its Q1 2026 earnings on April 21; Bank of America raised price target to $337 following CMS finalizing Medicare Advantage rates with a net +2.48% increase.
